stories.headline: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
stories.summary: US Central Command says the strikes were taken in "self-defense". It comes as senior Iranian negotiators are in Qatar for talks to end the war.

stories.headline: Iran war day 88: US attacks near Strait of Hormuz; talks under way in Qatar
stories.summary: US forces carry out series of attacks in the country's south as Iranian delegation holds talks in Qatar.

stories.headline: Rubio says Iran deal could take days after US forces launch new attacks
stories.summary: US forces say they hit Iranian missile sites and boats as Iranian delegation in Qatar for talks on extending ceasefire.

stories.headline: US strikes Iranian missile sites as peace talks continue
stories.summary: Washington announces ‘self-defence’ operation against targets in southern Iran

stories.headline: Oil Rebounds as US Launches New Strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Oil rebounded as fresh US military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ‘Lukashenka will fulfil all orders of Putin,’ Tsikhanouskaya says amid threats from Minsk and Moscow
stories.summary: Amid Kyiv’s warning that Belarus could be dragged deeper into Russia’s war, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomed the Belarusian opposition leader in Kyiv for her first official visit just days after Moscow’s massive attack.

stories.headline: Iran to open Hormuz 30 days after US deal to end fighting: source

stories.summary: Before the US strikes on targets in southern Iran, Tehran said a deal with Washington was not imminent.

stories.headline: US strikes southern Iran in ‘self defence’ targeting Iranian mine boats
stories.summary: The US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats.

stories.headline: US military launches strikes on southern Iran amid talks in Qatar
stories.summary: US Central Command announces 'self-defence' strikes as Tehran's top negotiators gather in Qatar for talks.

stories.headline: Canada’s Mark Carney calls treatment of Gaza flotilla activists ‘appalling’
stories.summary: The prime minister called for an independent investigation into Israeli mistreatment, but stopped short of penalties.

stories.summary: Attacks threaten US-Iran peace negotiations as Tehran says US Reaper drone 'downed' over its territory.

stories.headline: Tuesday briefing: With unease at home spreading, what next for Russia’s isolated leader?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: Our Russian affairs reporter on Vladimir Putin’s slipping approval and singular goal – as discontent ripples from wider society to the reachers of the KremlinGood morning. There is little doubt that when Vladimir Putin ordered his invasion of Ukraine in 2022, he did not expect his troops to still be embroiled there in 2026. And he surely never envisaged a scaled-down victory parade in Moscow, stripped of military hardware,

stories.headline: Dozens of drones fall from sky during Sydney festival show
stories.summary: A major drone show at Sydney’s Vivid festival was cancelled after nearly 90 drones malfunctioned and fell into Darling Harbour during a live performance.

stories.headline: Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave
stories.summary: It comes after the Ukrainian capital suffered one of the biggest aerial assaults of the war overnight on Saturday.

stories.headline: Australia confirms first diphtheria death amid worst outbreak in decades
stories.summary: Most cases are in the Northern Territory with some also in Western Australia, South Australia and Queensland.

stories.headline: Dozens of drones crash into Sydney harbour after light show glitch
stories.summary: Vivid Sydney organisers and the UK company behind the drone show blamed it on technical difficulties.

stories.headline: Watch: Bridge collapses and car swept away in China river
stories.summary: The driver and passengers of the car managed to escape before the bridge gave way in Xiaogan.

stories.headline: ByteDance offers AI team special stock to fend off poaching
stories.summary: TikTok owner issues shares tied to AI business unit as China’s tech talent war heats up

stories.headline: JPMorgan Says Stocks Buoyed by Prospect of Public, Private Spending
stories.summary: Karen Ward, JPMorgan Asset Management's EMEA chief market strategist, says that investors are looking beyond the current geopolitical uncertainty to the investment opportunities resulting from increased public and private spending. "The more chaotic the world becomes, the more that's creating spending," Ward tells Bloomberg Television. "I think that's really what markets are focused on. Whatever happens in the next month or two, there's a much bi

stories.headline: Danish Central Bank Warns of Housing Risks as Price Gains Spread
stories.summary: A surge in Copenhagen home prices is spilling over to the rest of Denmark, posing growing risks to the country’s lenders, the central bank said.

stories.headline: A runoff in Texas primary will test Trump’s grip on GOP
stories.summary: The president's candidate, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, has faced numerous scandals, while Sen. John Cornyn has failed to excite the base.

stories.summary: Bureau of Meteorology says thunderstorms could bring widespread rainfalls and flash flooding while there are ‘signs of El Niño development’Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastSevere thunderstorms could bring heavy rainfall and flash flooding to eastern and south-eastern Australia in coming days, according to the weather bureau.On Tuesday afternoon, the Bureau of Meteorol

stories.headline: Tensions rise in Bolivia as protesters march toward presidential palace
stories.summary: Demonstrators gathered near the presidential palace in La Paz as police maintained blockades and tensions remained high.

stories.headline: Japan risks losing out without drug pricing reform, warns AstraZeneca CEO
SpaceX to drive a Cybertruck through corporate governance norms
stories.summary: Provisions in the IPO prospectus will give Elon Musk more freedom — and potentially less accountability

stories.headline: Taiwan Overtakes India as World's Fifth-Largest Stock Market
stories.summary: Taiwan overtook India in stock market value, powered mainly by a breakneck rally in the world’s largest chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

stories.headline: Japan Set to Secure More Oil Imports in May After April Slump
stories.summary: Japan’s May crude oil imports are expected to rebound to around 1.7 million barrels a day, roughly three-quarters of the level a year earlier, as refiners step up alternative procurement while the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

stories.headline: Palm Fruit Left to Rot as Indonesia Export Revamp Hits Farmers
stories.summary: Some palm oil refiners in Indonesia are avoiding buying fruit from small farmers, a sign of how the government’s commodity export overhaul risks squeezing grower incomes and crimping supplies of the crop.

stories.headline: Indonesia Probes Major Palm Oil Companies on Export Price Issues
stories.summary: Indonesia is probing some of the world’s biggest palm-oil companies for suspected export abuses, adding to the upheaval in the industry days after the government announced a plan to nationalize shipments of the crop.

stories.headline: MUFG Is Said to Explore Options for Bank Danamon Stake
stories.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. is exploring options for PT Bank Danamon Indonesia, including a take-private of the lender or selling some its stake to increase the public float, people with knowledge of the matter said.

stories.headline: Ethio Telecom Lists on Bourse, Marking Ethiopia’s First IPO
stories.summary: Ethio telecom listed on Ethiopia’s nascent stock market more than a year since the close of its initial public offering and marking the nation’s maiden IPO.

stories.headline: Malaysia Jolts Bullion Trade With 10% Import Duty on Gold Bars
stories.summary: Malaysia has imposed a 10% import duty on some gold bar shipments, according to traders familiar with the matter, disrupting the bullion trade in the Southeast Asian nation.

stories.headline: China Lets Policy Loan Rate Fall to Record Low to Boost Economy
stories.summary: China let the interest rate on a one-year policy loan to banks decline to a record low, according to people familiar with the matter, a sign Beijing is stepping up efforts to support an economy that’s losing momentum.

stories.headline: China Chip Stocks Jump on Hopes for Huawei Tech | The China Show 5/26/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ESAI's Emerson: More Upside Pressure for Energy Prices
stories.summary: ESAI Energy Founder & President Sarah Emerson believes energy prices will come under further pressure until physical oil supplies successfully navigate global choke points, like the Strait of Hormuz, and restock consumer inventories. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ASX Posts Worst Selloff Ever After Revising Up Tech Revamp Costs
stories.summary: ASX Ltd. shares notched their steepest drop on record after the embattled exchange operator increased its capital spending guidance as it upgrades critical market infrastructure.

stories.headline: Seven die in heatwave-related incidents in France – Europe live
stories.summary: Deaths include five drownings The French government said Tuesday that seven people were reported to have died in connection to the recent heatwave baking much of western Europe, five of which were drownings, AFP reported.“What I can say today is that there have been seven deaths directly or indirectly related to the heat,” government spokesperson Maud Bregeon told television broadcaster TF1. stories.headline: ICE agents use pepper spray on protesters outside detention centre
stories.summary: Dozens of ICE agents clashed with protesters using pepper spray to disperse crowds outside a detention centre.

stories.headline: Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force.

stories.headline: Istanbul’s Bilgi University reopened after police crackdown
stories.summary: Students celebrated after days of protests against the closure decree that would have shut Bilgi University mid-year.

stories.headline: Peru clowns fill Lima streets for annual Day of Laughter parade
stories.summary: Peru celebrated its annual Clown Day on 25 May as hundreds of performers in red noses and colourful wigs paraded through the historic centre of Lima, turning the Plaza de Armas into a lively stage of music.

stories.headline: Can heat pumps cool down your home and how do they compare to air conditioning units?
stories.summary: Europe consumes huge amounts of electricity and generates tonnes of CO2 through cooling. Could heat pumps save us during the next heatwave?

stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.

stories.headline: Iran is beating Trump at the art of the deal
stories.summary: The war could end with Tehran more confident, more hardline and with new resources to rebuild its nuclear programme

stories.headline: Moment of gas explosion at China coal mine
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

stories.headline: Oil Rebounds as Strikes Near Hormuz Muddy Outlook for Iran Deal
stories.summary: Oil rebounded as fresh US military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with talks set to continue for several more days.

stories.headline: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
stories.summary: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate actionThe World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease.“We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as

stories.headline: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
stories.summary: Some speculate that El Malick Ndiaye stepped down so that ousted-PM Ousmane Sonko can take his place.

stories.headline: Will Maureen Galindo’s ‘Zionists’ Comments Matter in Texas House Runoff Election?
stories.summary: Democratic leaders accused Maureen Galindo of antisemitism and are trying to stop her from winning the party’s primary in a contested U.S. House district.

stories.headline: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
stories.summary: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some woundedInmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners.“We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video

stories.headline: Cambodia's former opposition leader receives royal pardon for 27-year sentence
stories.summary: Kem Sokha was serving a 27-year sentence on treason charges, which were widely derided as politically motivated.

stories.headline: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment
stories.summary: The prisoners insisted their protest was peaceful but that security forces deployed to quell it opened fire.

stories.headline: Brexit red tape costs hit food firms
stories.summary: The complaints come as ministers consider closer alignment with EU food rules to ease delays.

stories.headline: Trump’s Self-Indulgent Streak Deepens G.O.P. Fears He Is Risking Losses in Midterms
stories.summary: “The stupid stuff is killing our chances,” said a retiring Republican senator.

stories.headline: Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows
stories.summary: More than 20,000 attacks on markets, farmland and food distribution systems have been recorded since 2018Hunger is being increasingly exploited as a weapon of war with more than 20,000 documented incidents of “food-related violence” in the past eight years, new analysis reveals.Attacks include 1,261 strikes on markets used by families for daily groceries and 863 incidents in which food distribution systems were targeted and workers killed. stories.headline: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
stories.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

stories.headline: Turkish riot police storm opposition offices after leaders ousted
stories.summary: Officers forced their way into the building days after the party vowed to defy a court ruling removing its leaders.

stories.headline: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
stories.summary: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
stories.summary: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi provinceAt least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years.The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. stories.headline: Rosenberg: Luhansk strike sparks Russian accusations and vow to retaliate
stories.summary: Eighteen people were killed and 42 others injured in the strike on the town in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

stories.headline: UK officials suggested single market for goods with Europe
stories.summary: Industry figures briefed on the move said the idea has not been taken forward at this point, after EU scepticism.

stories.headline: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief

stories.headline: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
stories.summary: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence.Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the

stories.headline: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation effortsWhen the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action.“The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuationsHeavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of

stories.headline: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

stories.headline: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.headline: ‘Long live Raúl!’ - Castro’s supporters in Cuba rally in his defence
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant reports from a pro-government demonstration held after the US indictment of the former president.

stories.headline: The deadly plane attack at the centre of Castro's indictment
stories.summary: Raúl Castro was armed forces minister when Cuban military jets shot down two civilian planes operated by exiles of the communist country.

stories.headline: Watch key moments from BBC's interview with pardoned Honduran ex-president
stories.summary: Juan Orlando Hernández was controversially pardoned by Trump in December while serving a 45-year prison sentence in the US for drug trafficking.
